description: Comprehensive button/control reference data extracted from the official Octatrack MK2 manual (OS 1.40A). Used for interactive tooltips on the SVG overlay.
source: Octatrack MKII User Manual, April 14 2021, OS version 1.40A

proj.label: PROJECT
proj.primary: Opens the PROJECT menu where sets, projects, system settings, MIDI config, mixer, and tempo are accessed. This is the master settings hub of the Octatrack.
proj.func_combo: [FUNC] + [PROJ] = Quick-save the current project (equivalent to PROJECT > SAVE).
all_combos: [FUNC] + [PROJ] = Save project
proj.menu_path: PROJECT menu → PROJECT (Load/Save/Reload/Export/Purge) | SYSTEM (USB/OS/Date/Personalize/Card Tools/Status) | CONTROL (Audio/Input/Sequencer/MIDI Seq/Memory/Metronome) | MIDI (Control/Sync/Channels/Turbo) | MIXER | TEMPO
parameters: PROJECT: Change, Save, Reload, Sync To Card, Save To New, Export To Set, Collect Samples, Purge Samples, Save Current Bank, Reload Current Bank
SYSTEM: USB Disk Mode, OS Upgrade, Date/Time, Personalize, Card Tools, Status

CONTROL: Audio (Track 8 master/normal, CUE config), Input (Noise gates, Delay compensate), Sequencer (Chain After, Silence Tracks, LFO Auto Change), MIDI Sequencer (CC Direct Connect), Memory (Flex format, Dynamic recorders, Reserve length), Metronome
MIDI: Control (Audio CC In/Out, Audio Note In/Out), Sync (Transport/Clock Send/Receive, Prog Ch), Channels (Auto channel, Trig channels), Turbo Status
MIXER: MIX (headphone blend), Main/Cue Out levels, AB/CD DIR, AB/CD Gain, Mute
TEMPO: BPM setting, Project/Pattern tempo mode
tips: Always save your project (FUNC+PROJ) before switching projects or ejecting the CF card.
SYNC TO CARD should be performed before removing the CF card to avoid data corruption.
SAVE TO NEW is essential when experimenting — it preserves your working template.
PURGE SAMPLES removes unused samples from Flex/Static slot lists only, not from the Audio Pool.

part.label: PART
part.primary: Opens the PART QUICK SELECT menu where you link one of 4 parts to the active pattern. Parts contain machine assignments, sample assignments, effect assignments, track parameters, and 16 scenes.
part.func_combo: [FUNC] + [PART] = Opens the PART EDIT pop-up menu (Rename, Reload, Save, Save All).
all_combos: [FUNC] + [PART] = Part Edit menu (Rename/Reload/Save/Save All)
[FUNC] + [CUE] = Reload active part to saved settings (Part Reload)
part.menu_path: PART QUICK SELECT → Select Part 1-4 for the active pattern. PART EDIT (via FUNC) → Rename / Reload / Save / Save All.
parameters: 4 parts per bank (64 parts per project)
Each part holds: machine assignments, sample slot assignments, FX assignments, track parameter settings, 16 scenes
part.leds: No dedicated LED. The active part name is shown on screen below the tempo display.
tips: Patterns sharing the same Part also share machine assignments — edit one and you edit both!
Parts are PER-BANK: 4 parts x 16 banks = 64 parts per project.
An asterisk (*) next to a part name means it has been modified but not saved.
Quick-link a part by holding [FUNC] while selecting it in the PART menu.
Press [FUNC] + [CUE] to reload a part to its saved state — great for live recovery after heavy tweaking.

aed.label: AUDIO EDITOR
aed.primary: Opens the Audio Editor for the sample assigned to the active track's machine. The Audio Editor has 5 sub-pages: Trim, Slice, Edit, Attributes, and File.
aed.func_combo: [FUNC] + [AED] = Opens the CREATE SLICE GRID menu directly (shortcut to slicing).
all_combos: [FUNC] + [AED] = Open CREATE SLICE GRID menu (shortcut)
[TRACK] + [BANK] = Open the track's assigned sample in the Audio Editor
[REC1/2] + [BANK] = Open the recorder buffer of the active track in the Audio Editor
[FUNC] + [BANK] (in sample slot list) = Open selected sample in Audio Editor
aed.menu_path: AUDIO EDITOR → TRIM ([SRC]) | SLICE ([AMP]) | EDIT ([LFO]) | ATTRIBUTES ([FX1]) | FILE ([FX2])
parameters: TRIM: Start point, Loop point, End point, Waveform zoom/scroll
SLICE: Add/Delete slices, Create Slice Grid (2-64 slices), Create Linear/Random Locks, Reverse/Normalize slices
EDIT: Select All, Crop, Delete, Save Selection, Reverse, Fade In/Out, Normalize, +3dB/-3dB, Silence, Copy/Paste, Duplicate, Mix Channels, Invert, Calculate BPM
ATTRIBUTES: Gain, Loop Mode (Off/On/Pingpong), Timestretch (Off/Normal/Beat), Original Tempo, Trim Len (bars), Loop Len (bars), Quantized Trig
FILE: Load New Sample, Save Sample Settings, Save Sample Copy, Save and Assign, Clear Slot, Revert to Saved
tips: Hold [FUNC] while moving start/end/loop markers to snap to zero-amplitude crossings — prevents clicks.
Press [FUNC] + [YES] to preview the sample from the main outputs; [CUE] + [YES] for cue outputs.
Sample edits on Flex samples are session-only! Save them via FILE or they're lost on reboot.
A blinking star next to a sample name in the slot list indicates unsaved edits.
Set ORIGINAL TEMPO correctly in Attributes for time-stretching to work properly.

mix.label: MIXER
mix.primary: Opens the MIXER menu where headphone mix, main/cue output levels, input DIR levels, input GAIN, and track mute/solo are configured. Settings here are global to all patterns in the project.
all_combos: [FUNC] + [MIX] = Toggle metronome on/off
[CUE] + [TEMPO] = Toggle metronome on/off (alternative shortcut)
mix.menu_path: MIXER menu → MIX (headphone blend) | MAIN OUT | CUE OUT | AB DIR | CD DIR | AB GAIN | CD GAIN | MUTE (trig keys 1-8 audio, 9-16 MIDI)
parameters: MIX: Headphone blend between main output mirror (left) and cue-only (right)
MAIN/CUE OUT: Final output level (+12dB max boost at +63, 0 = unity)
AB/CD DIR: Routes raw input audio to main outputs (0=off, 127=unity). Does NOT affect sampling volume.
AB/CD GAIN: Gain/attenuate input signal (-64=mute, 0=unity, +63=+12dB). Affects sampling, DIR, and Thru machines.
MUTE: Trig keys 1-8 = audio track mutes, 9-16 = MIDI track mutes
mix.leds: No dedicated LED. Muted tracks shown as unlit [TRIG] keys in the Mixer; solo'ed tracks as green.
tips: When not in the Mixer menu, press [FUNC] + LEVEL knob to quickly adjust the MAIN output volume.
DIR sound bypasses track effects but IS affected by the master track if Track 8 is set as master.
Solo tracks by pressing [CUE] + [TRIG] keys while in the Mixer menu.
Hold [FUNC] while pressing mute [TRIG] keys to queue multiple mute changes — they all take effect when you release [FUNC].
If Neighbor machines are used, you can only mute the last Neighbor in the chain, not preceding tracks.

arr.label: ARRANGER
arr.primary: Opens the ARRANGER menu where arrangements (up to 256 rows of sequenced patterns) are edited, renamed, saved, and chained.
arr.func_combo: [FUNC] + [ARR] = Toggle ARRANGEMENT mode on/off. When lit, the Arranger controls playback.
all_combos: [FUNC] + [ARR] = Toggle Arrangement mode on/off
arr.menu_path: ARRANGER menu → EDIT | RENAME | CHANGE | CHAIN | CLEAR | SAVE | RELOAD
parameters: ROW: Up to 256 rows per arrangement
PAT: Pattern assignment per row (also HALT, LOOP, JUMP, REM commands)
REP: Number of repeats per row
OF: Pattern offset (start from specific step)
LN: Override pattern length
SCENE: Per-row scene A/B assignments
T: MIDI track transpose per row
B: Per-row BPM override
M: Per-row track mutes
arr.leds: [ARR] key lights up when ARRANGEMENT mode is active.
tips: 8 arrangements per project — use CHANGE to switch between them.
LOOP commands can be nested for complex song structures.
Arrangement tempo settings override any pattern or project tempo settings.
Insert rows with [FUNC] + [DOWN], remove with [FUNC] + [UP].
Copy/paste/clear/undo functions are available in the Arrangement Edit menu.

midi.label: MIDI
midi.primary: Activates MIDI editing mode. When lit, the 8 tracks now represent the 8 MIDI tracks for sequencing external MIDI gear. Press again to return to audio track mode.
midi.func_combo: [FUNC] + [MIDI] = Opens the MIDI SYNC settings (Transport/Clock Send/Receive, Program Change).
all_combos: [FUNC] + [MIDI] = Open MIDI Sync settings
[MIDI] + [UP]/[DOWN] = Select MIDI MAP configuration per track (when Audio Note In = MAP/TRACK)
midi.menu_path: MIDI mode: Track Parameter pages become NOTE MAIN/SETUP, ARP MAIN/SETUP, LFO MAIN/SETUP, CTRL1 MAIN/SETUP, CTRL2 MAIN/SETUP
parameters: NOTE: Note, Velocity, Length, Notes 2-4 (chords)
NOTE SETUP: MIDI Channel, Bank, Program, Sub-Bank
ARP: Transpose, Legato, Mode (Off/True/Up/Down/Cycl/Shfl/Random), Speed, Range, Note Length
ARP SETUP: Arpeggio length (up to 16 steps), note offsets, key scale
LFO: Same as audio LFOs but exclusive to MIDI tracks
CTRL 1: Pitch Bend, Aftertouch, CC1-CC4
CTRL 2: CC5-CC10
CTRL SETUP: CC assignment with MIDI Learn ([FUNC] + knob)
midi.leds: [MIDI] key is lit when MIDI editing mode is active. Two pixels in screen upper-left show MIDI port activity (left=receive, right=send).
tips: MIDI track parameters cannot be assigned to scenes.
Each MIDI track sends chords of up to 4 notes with adjustable velocity and length.
Use MIDI CC Learn ([FUNC] + knob C-F in CTRL SETUP) to quickly assign external synth knob CCs.
Avoid assigning the same MIDI channels to both audio and MIDI tracks to prevent conflicts.
When MIDI mode is active and not recording, [TRIG 1-8] trig audio tracks and [TRIG 9-16] trig MIDI tracks.

func.label: FUNCTION
func.primary: Modifier key — press and hold to access secondary functions of other buttons. Secondary functions are printed in gray text on the front panel. Also used for quick parameter value jumps when held while turning encoders.
all_combos: [FUNC] + [PROJ] = Save project
[FUNC] + [PART] = Part Edit menu
[FUNC] + [AED] = Create Slice Grid
[FUNC] + [MIX] = Toggle metronome
[FUNC] + [ARR] = Toggle Arrangement mode
[FUNC] + [MIDI] = MIDI Sync settings
[FUNC] + [TRACK] = Mute/unmute track
[FUNC] + [CUE] = Reload active part
[FUNC] + [PTN] = Pattern Settings menu
[FUNC] + [BANK] = Track Trig Edit menu (in Grid Rec) / Pattern Settings (outside Grid Rec)
[FUNC] + [PAGE] = Scale Setup menu
[FUNC] + [TEMPO] = Tap Tempo (tap repeatedly)
[FUNC] + [RECORD] = Copy
[FUNC] + [PLAY] = Clear
[FUNC] + [STOP] = Paste
[FUNC] + [SRC/AMP/LFO/FX1/FX2] = Open Setup page for that track parameter
[FUNC] + [REC1] = Recording Setup 1 menu
[FUNC] + [REC2] = Recording Setup 2 menu
[FUNC] + [REC3] = Recording Edit menu
[FUNC] + [SCENE A/B] = Mute scene A or B
[FUNC] + [TRIG] = Place lock trig / one shot trig (in Grid Recording)
[FUNC] + [TRIG 9-16] = Manually trig trigless trigs (when not recording, in Tracks/Chromatic mode)
[FUNC] + [UP]/[DOWN] = Quick scroll in lists / Change active Trig mode
[FUNC] + [LEFT]/[RIGHT] = Shift all trigs forward/backward (in Grid Recording) / Change octave (in Chromatic mode)
[FUNC] + [YES] = Preview sample from main outputs / Arm all one-shot trigs
[FUNC] + [NO] = Remove parameter locks in Live Recording / Disarm one-shot trigs / Erase characters in naming
[FUNC] + LEVEL = Adjust MAIN output volume (global)
[FUNC] + encoder turn = Parameter value jump (snap to 0, 64, 127 etc.)
func.menu_path: N/A — modifier key, accesses secondary functions across all menus.
tips: FUNC is a modifier key — it does nothing alone. Always used in combination with another button.
Holding FUNC while turning a parameter encoder makes values jump to key positions (0, 64, 127, etc.).
The gray text printed below many buttons on the panel shows what FUNC + that button does.
FUNC + encoder press is used in some contexts (e.g., FUNC + DATA ENTRY knob press to enable/disable MIDI CC parameters).

cue.label: CUE
cue.primary: Modifier key for routing tracks to the cue/headphone outputs. Press [CUE] + [TRACK] to cue a track. Also used for previewing samples from cue outputs and setting cue levels.
cue.func_combo: [FUNC] + [CUE] = Reload the active part to its saved settings (Part Reload).
all_combos: [CUE] + [TRACK] = Route track to cue outputs (track key flashes when cued)
[CUE] + [REC1/2/3] = Route audio inputs to cue outputs
[CUE] + LEVEL knob = Set cue level for the active track
[CUE] + [YES] = Preview sample from cue outputs (in Audio Editor, sample lists, file browser)
[CUE] + [TRIG] (in Mixer menu) = Solo track
[CUE] + [TEMPO] = Toggle metronome on/off
[CUE] + [NO] = Erase character in naming menu without opening pop-up
[FUNC] + [CUE] = Reload active part to saved settings
cue.menu_path: N/A — modifier key for cue routing and preview operations.
cue.leds: No dedicated LED. Cued tracks indicated by flashing [TRACK] keys. Cued inputs shown by alternating <REC> LEDs.
tips: A cued track still plays on the main outputs too — cueing adds it to cue, doesn't remove from main.
Enable CUE MUTES TRACK in Personalize to auto-mute a track from main when cueing it.
In STUDIO mode (PROJECT > CONTROL > AUDIO), CUE becomes an independent assignable output pair.
CUE output is NOT available on Track 8 if it's set as the master track.
The headphone MIX parameter in the Mixer menu controls the blend between main and cue signals.

ptn.label: PATTERN
ptn.primary: Press [PTN] + a [TRIG 1-16] key to select a pattern within the current bank. You have 4 seconds to make the selection. Green TRIG keys = patterns with data, red = active pattern.
ptn.func_combo: [FUNC] + [PTN] = Opens the PATTERN SETTINGS menu (scale mode, track play settings, silence tracks, etc.).
all_combos: [PTN] + [TRIG 1-16] = Select pattern within current bank
[FUNC] + [PTN] = Open Pattern Settings menu
Hold [PTN] + press multiple [TRIG] keys in sequence = Pattern Chain (loop a sequence of patterns)
ptn.menu_path: PATTERN SETTINGS menu (via FUNC+PTN) → PATTERN: Scale Mode (Normal/Per Track), Chain Behavior | TRACK 1-8: Start Silent, Plays Free, Oneshot Track, Trig Mode, Trig Quant
parameters: SCALE MODE: Normal (shared length) or Per Track (individual lengths)
CHAIN BEHAVIOR: Pattern length or custom step count before next chained pattern
Per-track: START SILENT (Auto/No/Yes), PLAYS FREE, ONESHOT TRACK, TRIG MODE (One/One2/Hold), TRIG QUANT
ptn.leds: When [PTN] is pressed, [TRIG] keys show: green = patterns with data, red = active pattern, unlit = empty patterns.
tips: Pattern chaining: hold [PTN] and press TRIG keys in order — patterns loop in that sequence.
Chaining only works within the same bank and cannot contain the same pattern twice.
Press [NO] to cancel pattern selection if you change your mind within the 4-second timeout.
Each pattern is linked to one of 4 Parts — changing patterns may change Parts and all machine settings.

bank.label: BANK
bank.primary: Press [BANK] + [TRIG 1-16] to select a bank (A-P). After selecting a bank, you're prompted to select a pattern. This is the quickest way to jump between banks and patterns.
bank.func_combo: [FUNC] + [BANK] = Opens TRACK TRIG EDIT menu (when Grid Recording is active) showing Trigs/Slide/Swing/Recorder trigs. Outside Grid Recording, opens PATTERN SETTINGS menu.
all_combos: [BANK] + [TRIG 1-16] = Select bank A through P
[FUNC] + [BANK] = Track Trig Edit menu (in Grid Rec) / Pattern Settings (otherwise)
[FUNC] + [BANK] (in sample slot list or SRC Setup) = Open selected sample in Audio Editor
[FUNC] + [BANK] (in Arranger) = Access arranger options
[FUNC] + [BANK] (in Parts menu) = Access part options
[FUNC] + [BANK] (in Project Change) = Rename/Delete project
[TRACK] + [BANK] = Open track's sample in Audio Editor
[REC1/2] + [BANK] = Open recorder buffer in Audio Editor
bank.menu_path: TRACK TRIG EDIT menu (via FUNC+BANK in Grid Rec) → TRIGS | SLIDE | SWING | REC TRIGS
parameters: TRIGS: Shows/edits sample trigs, lock trigs, trigless trigs, one shot trigs
SLIDE: Shows/edits slide trigs (gradual parameter value transitions)
SWING: Shows/edits swing trigs + swing amount (LEVEL knob)
REC TRIGS: Shows/edits recorder trigs + source selection per trig
bank.leds: When [BANK] is pressed, [TRIG] keys show: green = banks with data, red = active bank.
tips: 16 banks x 16 patterns = 256 patterns per project.
Bank switching is seamless — playback won't halt or cut audio.
The Track Trig Edit menu gives you a complete visual overview of all trig types on the active track.
When copying a track with Track Trig Edit open, only trigs are copied (no machine/FX assignments).

yes_btn.label: YES
yes_btn.primary: Confirm selections, enter sub-menus, and assign options. Also arms one-shot trigs: in Grid Recording arms the active track's one-shot sample trigs; outside Grid Recording arms ALL one-shot trigs on all tracks.
yes_btn.func_combo: [FUNC] + [YES] = Preview sample from main outputs (in Audio Editor, sample lists, file browser). Also arms one-shot trigs when YES arming is disabled in Personalize.
all_combos: [YES] = Confirm / Enter sub-menu / Arm one-shot trigs
[FUNC] + [YES] = Preview sample from main outs / Arm one-shot trigs (when direct arm disabled)
[CUE] + [YES] = Preview sample from cue outputs
[TRIG] + [YES] = Preview audio track trig on MAIN outputs
[TRACK] + [YES] = Re-arm one-shot recorder trigs for that track
[TRACK PARAMETER] + [YES] = Randomize all parameters on that track parameter page
tips: In GRID RECORDING mode, [YES] arms only the active track's one-shot sample trigs (shows 'ARM TRK').
Outside GRID RECORDING, [YES] arms ALL one-shot trigs on ALL tracks (shows 'ARM ALL').
YES/NO arm/disarm can be disabled in Personalize if you accidentally trigger it during performance.
In the Track Trig Edit TRIGS page, [YES] quantizes micro-timed trigs to ~50% of their offset. Press 6 times to fully quantize.

no_btn.label: NO
no_btn.primary: Exit active menus, deselect options, and cancel operations. Also disarms one-shot trigs: in Grid Recording disarms the active track's one-shot sample trigs; outside Grid Recording disarms ALL one-shot trigs.
no_btn.func_combo: [FUNC] + [NO] = Remove all parameter locks from the active track in LIVE RECORDING mode. Also disarms one-shot trigs when direct arm is disabled. Erases characters in naming menus.
all_combos: [NO] = Exit menu / Cancel / Disarm one-shot trigs
[FUNC] + [NO] = Remove parameter locks in Live Recording / Disarm trigs / Erase characters in naming
[CUE] + [NO] = Erase characters in naming without opening pop-up
[TRIG] + [NO] = Convert trig type: sample trig→trigless trig→lock trig (cycle)
[NO] + [TRACK] = Erase trigs from that track in LIVE RECORDING mode
[NO] + knob push = Erase specific parameter locks in LIVE RECORDING mode
[NO] + LEVEL push = Remove sample locks in LIVE RECORDING mode
[TRACK] + [NO] = Disarm one-shot recorder trigs for that track
tips: All windows and menus can be closed with [NO].
[TRIG] + [NO] cycles through trig types: sample trig → trigless trig → lock trig → (press TRIG to make sample trig again).
In LIVE RECORDING: [NO] + [TRACK] erases all trigs hit by the running sequencer on that track.
YES/NO arm/disarm of one-shot trigs can be disabled in the Personalize menu to avoid accidental triggering.

arrow_up.label: UP
arrow_up.primary: Navigate upward in menus and lists. Adjusts parameter values in some menus (e.g., naming, date/time). Also used with [FUNC] for quick scrolling and changing Trig modes.
arrow_up.func_combo: [FUNC] + [UP] = Quick scroll (page up) in lists. When no scrollable menu is open, changes the active TRIG mode.
all_combos: [FUNC] + [UP] = Quick scroll up / Change Trig mode
[TRIG] + [UP] = Open Sample Lock menu (in Grid Recording)
[UP] + [PAGE] = Activate FILL mode (held = active while held)
[UP] + [DOWN] + [PAGE] = Latch FILL mode
tips: Quick scrolling ([FUNC]+[UP]) is temporarily disabled when previewing samples.
Use [UP] in the TEMPO menu to adjust the decimal part of the tempo.

arrow_down.label: DOWN
arrow_down.primary: Navigate downward in menus and lists. Adjusts parameter values in some menus.
arrow_down.func_combo: [FUNC] + [DOWN] = Quick scroll (page down) in lists. When no scrollable menu is open, changes the active TRIG mode.
all_combos: [FUNC] + [DOWN] = Quick scroll down / Change Trig mode
[TRIG] + [DOWN] = Open Sample Lock menu (in Grid Recording)
[DOWN] + [PAGE] = Activate FILL mode for one pattern cycle
[FUNC] + [DOWN] (in Arranger Edit) = Insert arrangement row
tips: [FUNC] + [UP/DOWN] changes the active Trig mode when no menu list is open: Tracks → Chromatic → Slots → Slices → Quick Mute → Delay Control.

arrow_left.label: LEFT
arrow_left.primary: Navigate left in menus. When no menu is open, temporarily nudges the BPM DOWN by ~10% (releases when key is released). In naming menus, moves cursor left.
arrow_left.func_combo: [FUNC] + [LEFT] = Shift all trigs backward one step (in Grid Recording). Change octave down (in Chromatic mode). Rotate LFO designer/arpeggiator sequence.
all_combos: [LEFT] = Menu navigation / Tempo nudge down (no menu open)
[FUNC] + [LEFT] = Shift trigs backward (Grid Rec) / Octave down (Chromatic) / Rotate sequence
[TRIG] + [LEFT] = Open Micro Timing menu (in Grid Recording)
[TRACK] + [LEFT] = Nudge sample tempo down for that track
[FUNC] + [LEFT] (in sample slot list) = Load previous sample from same directory
tips: Tempo nudge (pressing LEFT/RIGHT with no menu open) is great for manually syncing to a turntable or external source.
The nudge is temporary — tempo reverts when the key is released.

arrow_right.label: RIGHT
arrow_right.primary: Navigate right in menus. When no menu is open, temporarily nudges the BPM UP by ~10% (releases when key is released). In naming menus, moves cursor right.
arrow_right.func_combo: [FUNC] + [RIGHT] = Shift all trigs forward one step (in Grid Recording). Change octave up (in Chromatic mode). Rotate LFO designer/arpeggiator sequence.
all_combos: [RIGHT] = Menu navigation / Tempo nudge up (no menu open)
[FUNC] + [RIGHT] = Shift trigs forward (Grid Rec) / Octave up (Chromatic) / Rotate sequence
[TRIG] + [RIGHT] = Open Micro Timing menu (in Grid Recording)
[TRACK] + [RIGHT] = Nudge sample tempo up for that track
[FUNC] + [RIGHT] (in sample slot list) = Load next sample from same directory
tips: In the file browser, [RIGHT] opens folders and loads samples. [LEFT] goes back up the folder hierarchy.
Hold [TRACK] + [LEFT/RIGHT] to nudge the tempo of just that track's sample, independent of the sequencer.

t1.label: TRACK 1
t1.primary: Select Track 1 as the active track for editing. All parameter pages, encoders, and sequencer data now relate to Track 1.
all_combos: [FUNC] + [TRACK] = Mute/unmute track
[CUE] + [TRACK] = Cue track to headphone/cue outputs
[TRACK] + [REC1/2/3] = Initiate sampling to this track's recorder
[TRACK] + [PLAY] = Manually trig the track's machine
[TRACK] + [STOP] = Stop playback of the track's machine
[TRACK] + [BANK] = Open the track's sample in Audio Editor
[TRACK] + [YES] = Re-arm one-shot recorder trigs for this track
[TRACK] + [NO] = Disarm one-shot recorder trigs for this track
[TRACK] + [TEMPO] = Sync sequencer to Pickup machine on this track
[TRACK] + [LEFT/RIGHT] = Nudge sample tempo for this track
Double-press [TRACK] = Open Quick Assign menu
t1.leds: Lit = active track. Yellow = muted and active. Unlit = muted and not selected. Flashing = cued.
tips: Double-press a [TRACK] key to open the Quick Assign menu for fast machine/sample assignment.
Track mute status: bright = unmuted & active, dim = unmuted & not active, yellow = muted & active, unlit = muted & not active.
In the Personalize menu, you can set MUTE FOCUSES TRACK and CUE FOCUSES TRACK to auto-select tracks when muting/cueing.

t2.label: TRACK 2
t2.primary: Select Track 2 as the active track.
all_combos: Same combinations as Track 1, applied to Track 2.
t2.leds: Same LED behavior as all track keys.
tips: Same tips as Track 1.

t3.label: TRACK 3
t3.primary: Select Track 3 as the active track.
all_combos: Same combinations as Track 1, applied to Track 3.
t3.leds: Same LED behavior as all track keys.
tips: Same tips as Track 1.

t4.label: TRACK 4
t4.primary: Select Track 4 as the active track.
all_combos: Same combinations as Track 1, applied to Track 4.
t4.leds: Same LED behavior as all track keys.
tips: Same tips as Track 1.

t5.label: TRACK 5
t5.primary: Select Track 5 as the active track.
all_combos: Same combinations as Track 1, applied to Track 5.
t5.leds: Same LED behavior as all track keys.
tips: Same tips as Track 1. Neighbor machines CANNOT be assigned to Track 5 (no preceding track in the second block).

t6.label: TRACK 6
t6.primary: Select Track 6 as the active track.
all_combos: Same combinations as Track 1, applied to Track 6.
t6.leds: Same LED behavior as all track keys.
tips: Same tips as Track 1.

t7.label: TRACK 7
t7.primary: Select Track 7 as the active track.
all_combos: Same combinations as Track 1, applied to Track 7.
t7.leds: Same LED behavior as all track keys.
tips: Same tips as Track 1.

t8.label: TRACK 8
t8.primary: Select Track 8 as the active track. Track 8 can optionally be set as a MASTER track (PROJECT > CONTROL > AUDIO) which makes its effects process ALL audio including external inputs.
t8.func_combo: [FUNC] + [TRACK 8] = Mute/unmute Track 8 (unless it's a master track, which can't be muted).
all_combos: Same combinations as Track 1, applied to Track 8.
t8.leds: Same LED behavior as all track keys.
tips: When Track 8 is a master track: only AMP SETUP, LFO, and FX pages are available.
A master track cannot be muted and has no cue output.
Use Track 8 as master for 'master bus' processing: assign Compressor + EQ for global mix control.

src.label: SRC (Source)
src.primary: Opens the SRC MAIN page showing the source parameters for the active track's machine (PTCH, STRT, LEN, RATE, RTRG, RTIM for Flex/Static; INAB, VOL, INCD, VOL for Thru).
src.func_combo: [FUNC] + [SRC] = Opens SRC SETUP page where machines and samples are assigned. Also contains LOOP, SLIC, LEN, RATE, TSTR, TSNS settings.
all_combos: [SRC] = Open SRC MAIN page
[FUNC] + [SRC] = Open SRC SETUP page (machine/sample assignment)
Double-press [SRC] = Open SRC SETUP page
[SRC] (in Audio Editor) = Open TRIM sub-page
src.menu_path: SRC MAIN → Machine-specific parameters. SRC SETUP → Machine list + Sample slot list + Setup parameters (LOOP, SLIC, LEN, RATE, TSTR, TSNS).
parameters: FLEX/STATIC MAIN: PTCH (pitch), STRT (start/slice), LEN (length), RATE (speed), RTRG (retrig count), RTIM (retrig time)
FLEX/STATIC SETUP: LOOP (Auto/Off/On), SLIC (On/Off), LEN (Off/Time/Slic), RATE (affects timestretch or pitch), TSTR (Auto/Off/Normal/Beat), TSNS (transient sensitivity)
THRU MAIN: INAB, VOL(AB), INCD, VOL(CD)
PICKUP MAIN: PTCH, DIR (FWD/REV/PIPO), LEN (master/slave length), GAIN, OP (DUB/REPL)
src.leds: No dedicated LED. Active track parameter page shown on screen.
tips: STRT selects slices when SLIC=ON in the Setup page; otherwise it linearly scrubs through the sample.
Set TSTR to AUTO in Setup so each sample can have its own timestretch setting via Audio Editor Attributes.
RATE at negative values plays the sample backwards.
Integer PTCH changes = semitones. Max = +1 octave, min = -1 octave.

amp.label: AMP (Amplitude)
amp.primary: Opens the AMP MAIN page with amplitude envelope (ATK, HOLD, REL), volume (VOL), and stereo balance (BAL) parameters. Controls the volume shape of each trig.
amp.func_combo: [FUNC] + [AMP] = Opens AMP SETUP page with envelope behavior settings (AMP mode, SYNC, ATCK curve, FX1/FX2 envelope behavior).
all_combos: [AMP] = Open AMP MAIN page
[FUNC] + [AMP] = Open AMP SETUP page
Double-press [AMP] = Open AMP SETUP page
[AMP] (in Audio Editor) = Open SLICE sub-page
amp.menu_path: AMP MAIN → ATK, HOLD, REL, VOL, BAL, XVOL (scene only). AMP SETUP → AMP (ANLG/RTRG/R+T/TTRG), SYNC, ATCK (LIN/LOG), FX1, FX2.
parameters: AMP MAIN: ATK (attack time), HOLD (hold time in steps), REL (release time), VOL (pre-effects volume), BAL (stereo balance), XVOL (scene-only equal-energy volume)
AMP SETUP: AMP (envelope retrig mode), SYNC (BPM sync on/off), ATCK (linear/logarithmic curve), FX1/FX2 (filter/modulator envelope behavior)
tips: VOL is pre-effects; LEVEL (the knob) is post-effects. Both affect output volume differently.
HOLD parameter is expressed in sequencer steps and is BPM-dependent by default.
Set HOLD to infinite + use a keyboard: sound sustains as long as you hold the key.
XVOL is only visible when holding a [SCENE] key — it provides equal-energy fading to avoid center dip.
AMP SETUP is one of the few pages available on a master track.

lfo.label: LFO
lfo.primary: Opens the LFO MAIN page showing speed (SPD1-3) and depth (DEP1-3) for the track's three LFOs. LFOs are always tempo-synced.
lfo.func_combo: [FUNC] + [LFO] = Opens LFO SETUP page with detailed per-LFO settings (target parameter, waveform, multiplier, trigger mode) and the LFO Designer.
all_combos: [LFO] = Open LFO MAIN page
[FUNC] + [LFO] = Open LFO SETUP page
Double-press [LFO] = Open LFO SETUP page
[LFO] (in Audio Editor) = Open EDIT sub-page
lfo.menu_path: LFO MAIN → SPD1, SPD2, SPD3, DEP1, DEP2, DEP3. LFO SETUP → LFO1/LFO2/LFO3 pages (PMTR, WAVE, MULT, TRIG, SPD, DEP) + DESIGNER page.
parameters: Per LFO: PMTR (target parameter), WAVE (11 basic + 8 designer waveforms), MULT (speed multiplier), TRIG (FREE/TRIG/HOLD/ONE/HALF/SYNC TRIG/SYNC ONE/SYNC HALF), SPD (base speed), DEP (modulation depth)
LFO DESIGNER: 16-step custom waveform, stepped or interpolated, with randomize and invert options
tips: LFO3 can modulate LFO1 and LFO2's speed and depth (meta-modulation).
LFO2 can only modulate LFO1 (not LFO3). Modulation flows downward only.
For beat-synced LFO speeds: 16 = 1 bar, 32 = half bar, 64 = quarter note.
The LFO Designer is a mini-sequencer: draw 16-step rhythmic parameter changes.
Copy/paste LFO designer waveforms between tracks using the standard copy/paste commands.
Static machines cannot have their STRT parameter modulated by LFOs (use Flex for heavy STRT modulation).

fx1.label: FX1 (Effect 1)
fx1.primary: Opens the FX1 MAIN page showing the parameters of the effect assigned to the first effect slot. 10 effect types are available for FX1.
fx1.func_combo: [FUNC] + [FX1] = Opens FX1 SETUP page where you select and assign the effect, plus configure its setup parameters.
all_combos: [FX1] = Open FX1 MAIN page
[FUNC] + [FX1] = Open FX1 SETUP page (effect selection + setup)
Double-press [FX1] = Open FX1 SETUP page
[FX1] (in Audio Editor) = Open ATTRIBUTES sub-page
fx1.menu_path: FX1 MAIN → Effect-specific parameters. FX1 SETUP → Effect list + Setup parameters.
parameters: Available FX1 effects: 12/24dB Multi Mode Filter, 2-Band Parametric EQ, DJ-style Kill EQ, 2-10 Stage Phaser, Flanger, 2-10 Tap Chorus, Spatializer, Comb Filter, Dynamix Compressor, Lo-Fi Collection
tips: FX1 does NOT have delay or reverb options — those are FX2 only.
Chain Neighbor machines to get more than 2 effects per sound (up to 8 effects across 4 chained tracks).
FX1 parameters can be parameter-locked, scene-locked, and LFO-modulated.
The Multi Mode Filter has its own envelope, controlled by the FX1 setting in AMP SETUP.

fx2.label: FX2 (Effect 2)
fx2.primary: Opens the FX2 MAIN page showing the parameters of the effect assigned to the second effect slot. FX2 has 14 effect types including delays and reverbs.
fx2.func_combo: [FUNC] + [FX2] = Opens FX2 SETUP page where you select and assign the effect, plus configure its setup parameters.
all_combos: [FX2] = Open FX2 MAIN page
[FUNC] + [FX2] = Open FX2 SETUP page (effect selection + setup)
Double-press [FX2] = Open FX2 SETUP page
[FX2] (in Audio Editor) = Open FILE sub-page
fx2.menu_path: FX2 MAIN → Effect-specific parameters. FX2 SETUP → Effect list + Setup parameters.
parameters: Available FX2 effects: All 10 from FX1 PLUS: Echo Freeze Delay, Gatebox Plate Reverb, Spring Reverb, Dark Reverb
tips: Delays and reverbs are ONLY available in the FX2 slot.
The Echo Freeze Delay continues to ring out even after muting a track — press [STOP]+[STOP] to kill it.
Use the DELAY CONTROL trig mode for quick live delay time changes.
FX2 parameters can be parameter-locked, scene-locked, and LFO-modulated.

copy.label: RECORD / COPY
copy.primary: TRANSPORT: Toggles GRID RECORDING mode on/off (steady red light). Hold [RECORD] + press [PLAY] to enter LIVE RECORDING mode (flashing light). EDIT: [FUNC] + [RECORD] = COPY command.
copy.func_combo: [FUNC] + [RECORD] = Perform a COPY command. What is copied depends on the current context (pattern, track, trig, page, scene, etc.).
all_combos: [RECORD] = Toggle Grid Recording mode on/off
[RECORD] + [PLAY] = Enter Live Recording mode
[FUNC] + [RECORD] = Copy (context-dependent)
[PAGE] + [RECORD] = Copy active pattern page
[TRIG] + [RECORD] = Copy the selected trig
[SCENE] + [RECORD] = Copy the assigned scene
[TRACK PARAMETER] + [RECORD] = Copy track parameter page settings
copy.leds: Steady red = GRID RECORDING mode active. Flashing red = LIVE RECORDING mode active. Unlit = no recording mode.
tips: This is the same physical button for both RECORD (transport) and COPY (editing) functions!
In Grid Recording mode, the sequencer shows trigs for the active track. Outside Grid Rec, the last 8 [TRIG] keys flash to show trigs firing.
Copy operations are context-sensitive: outside Grid Rec copies the pattern, inside Grid Rec copies the track.
Live Recording mode records in real time: [TRIG 9-16] input sample trigs on tracks 1-8, and turning encoders records parameter locks.

clear.label: PLAY / CLEAR
clear.primary: TRANSPORT: Starts playback of the pattern or arrangement. Press again while playing to PAUSE. EDIT: [FUNC] + [PLAY] = CLEAR command.
clear.func_combo: [FUNC] + [PLAY] = Perform a CLEAR command. What is cleared depends on the current context. Clear operations can be undone by repeating the key combination.
all_combos: [PLAY] = Start/pause pattern playback
[RECORD] + [PLAY] = Enter Live Recording mode
[FUNC] + [PLAY] = Clear (context-dependent, undoable)
[PAGE] + [PLAY] = Clear active pattern page
[TRIG] + [PLAY] = Clear all parameter locks from selected trig
[SCENE] + [PLAY] = Clear assigned scene's locks
[TRACK PARAMETER] + [PLAY] = Initialize track parameter page to defaults
[TRACK] + [PLAY] = Manually trig the track's machine (play sample)
clear.leds: Green LED indicates the sequencer is playing.
tips: This is the same physical button for both PLAY (transport) and CLEAR (editing) functions!
All CLEAR operations can be UNDONE by pressing the same key combination again immediately.
[FUNC] + [PLAY] in the Recording Setup menus clears the recorder buffer of the active track.
After stopping, pressing [PLAY] restarts the pattern from the beginning.

paste.label: STOP / PASTE
paste.primary: TRANSPORT: Stops playback. Press [STOP] + [STOP] (double-stop) to kill all sound including delay tails and send All Notes Off on all MIDI tracks. EDIT: [FUNC] + [STOP] = PASTE command.
paste.func_combo: [FUNC] + [STOP] = Perform a PASTE command. Paste operations can be undone by repeating the key combination.
all_combos: [STOP] = Stop playback
[STOP] + [STOP] = Kill all sound, stop delays, send MIDI All Notes Off, re-arm all one-shot trigs
[FUNC] + [STOP] = Paste (context-dependent, undoable)
[PAGE] + [STOP] = Paste copied pattern page
[TRIG] + [STOP] = Paste copied trig
[SCENE] + [STOP] = Paste copied scene
[TRACK PARAMETER] + [STOP] = Paste copied track parameter page
[TRACK] + [STOP] = Stop playback of the track's machine
paste.leds: No dedicated play/stop LED (playback status shown on screen).
tips: This is the same physical button for both STOP (transport) and PASTE (editing) functions!
Double-stop ([STOP]+[STOP]) is the panic button — kills everything including delay tails.
Double-stop while sequencer is stopped also re-arms all one-shot trigs (can be disabled in Personalize).
All PASTE operations can be UNDONE by pressing the same key combination again immediately.
Pressing [STOP] while a pattern chain is playing resets the chain. A second [STOP] unlinks the chain entirely.

rec1.label: REC1 (Audio Record AB)
rec1.primary: Used for real-time sampling from external input pair A/B. Press [TRACK] + [REC1] to start recording from inputs A/B to the active track's recorder. The <REC> LED shows input signal strength.
rec1.func_combo: [FUNC] + [REC1] = Opens RECORDING SETUP 1 menu (INAB, INCD, RLEN, TRIG, SRC3, LOOP settings).
all_combos: [TRACK] + [REC1] = Start manual sampling from input AB to that track's recorder
[FUNC] + [REC1] = Open Recording Setup 1 menu
[CUE] + [REC1] = Cue/route input AB to cue outputs
[REC1] + [BANK] = Open recorder buffer of active track in Audio Editor
[REC1] (with Pickup machine) = Controls record/overdub function
rec1.menu_path: RECORDING SETUP 1 → INAB, INCD, RLEN, TRIG (ONE/ONE2/HOLD), SRC3, LOOP. Also shows recorder buffer bar with record/playback positions.
parameters: INAB: Input A/B source selection (-, A B, A, B, A+B)
INCD: Input C/D source selection (-, C D, C, D, C+D)
RLEN: Recording length in steps (or MAX for full buffer)
TRIG: Sampling behavior (ONE, ONE2, HOLD)
SRC3: Internal source (-, MAIN, CUE, T1-T8)
LOOP: Whether captured sample loops
rec1.leds: <REC1> LED indicates signal strength on input AB. When in Recording Setup, also indicates activated source selection for recorder trigs.
tips: With RECORD QUICK MODE enabled in Personalize, just pressing [REC1] alone starts sampling (no need to hold [TRACK]).
For Pickup machines, [REC1] becomes the record/overdub button and [REC2] becomes play/stop.
Press [REC1/2] + [BANK] to quickly open the recorder buffer in the Audio Editor.
Recorder buffers are VOLATILE — save them before powering off or changing projects!

rec2.label: REC2 (Audio Record CD)
rec2.primary: Used for real-time sampling from external input pair C/D. Press [TRACK] + [REC2] to start recording from inputs C/D to the active track's recorder.
rec2.func_combo: [FUNC] + [REC2] = Opens RECORDING SETUP 2 menu (FIN, FOUT, AB monitor, QREC, QPL, CD monitor).
all_combos: [TRACK] + [REC2] = Start manual sampling from input CD to that track's recorder
[FUNC] + [REC2] = Open Recording Setup 2 menu
[CUE] + [REC2] = Cue/route input CD to cue outputs
[REC2] + [BANK] = Open recorder buffer of active track in Audio Editor
[REC2] (with Pickup machine) = Controls play/stop function
rec2.menu_path: RECORDING SETUP 2 → FIN, FOUT, AB (monitor level), QREC, QPL, CD (monitor level).
parameters: FIN: Fade-in length for recording (in steps)
FOUT: Fade-out length for recording (in steps, added after recording ends)
AB: Pickup machine monitoring level for input AB
QREC: Quantize recording start (OFF/PLEN/1-64 steps)
QPL: Quantize manual playback start (OFF/PLEN/1-64 steps)
CD: Pickup machine monitoring level for input CD
rec2.leds: <REC2> LED indicates signal strength on input CD.
tips: Set FIN and FOUT to their lowest values (0.063) to avoid clicks when recorded samples loop.
QREC is essential for perfectly timed loop captures when synced to external gear.
QPL quantizes manual trig playback — great for keeping manually triggered loops in sync.

rec3.label: REC3 (Internal Record)
rec3.primary: Used for real-time sampling from internal sources (main output, cue output, or individual tracks). Press [TRACK] + [REC3] to start internal sampling to the active track's recorder.
rec3.func_combo: [FUNC] + [REC3] = Opens RECORDING EDIT menu (Edit This Recording, Save This Recording, Save All Recordings, Go To Memory Config).
all_combos: [TRACK] + [REC3] = Start internal sampling to that track's recorder
[FUNC] + [REC3] = Open Recording Edit menu (save/edit recordings)
rec3.menu_path: RECORDING EDIT menu → Edit This Recording (opens Audio Editor) | Save This Recording | Save All Recordings | Go To Memory Config.
parameters: The internal source is configured by SRC3 in Recording Setup 1 (-, MAIN, CUE, T1-T8)
rec3.leds: No signal-level LED for REC3. When in Recording Setup, a lit [REC3] indicates internal source is activated for a recorder trig.
tips: SRC3 = MAIN records the master output mix — perfect for resampling your entire beat.
SRC3 = CUE records only cue-routed tracks — use this for internal send effects.
SRC3 = T1-T8 records a specific track's output — great for bouncing down processed tracks.
Save recordings promptly! The RECORDING EDIT menu ([FUNC]+[REC3]) has save commands.

scene_a.label: SCENE A
scene_a.primary: Press [SCENE A] + [TRIG 1-16] to assign a scene to the A slot of the crossfader (fully active at leftmost crossfader position). Hold [SCENE A] and turn encoders to lock parameter values to that scene.
scene_a.func_combo: [FUNC] + [SCENE A] = Mute Scene A. Muted scene parameters are ignored, using the part's default values instead.
all_combos: [SCENE A] + [TRIG] = Assign scene to slot A
[SCENE A] + encoder turn = Lock parameter to Scene A
[SCENE A] + encoder press = Remove scene lock for that parameter
[SCENE A] + LEVEL turn = Lock XLV (equal-energy track level) to Scene A
[SCENE A] + LEVEL press = Remove XLV lock
[FUNC] + [SCENE A] = Mute/unmute Scene A
[SCENE A] + [RECORD] = Copy Scene A
[SCENE A] + [PLAY] = Clear Scene A locks
[SCENE A] + [STOP] = Paste to Scene A
scene_a.menu_path: N/A — scene assignment and locking key.
parameters: XLV: Equal-energy track level (MIN/MAX, scene-only)
XVOL: Equal-energy pre-FX volume (MIN/MAX, scene-only, on AMP page)
XDIR AB/CD: Equal-energy input DIR (MIN/MAX, scene-only, on Mixer page)
scene_a.leds: When [SCENE A] is pressed: red [TRIG] = currently assigned scene, green [TRIG] = Scene B's assignment, half-bright green = scenes containing locks.
tips: Scene locks override parameter locks — the crossfader always wins.
Use XLV/XVOL/XDIR for equal-energy crossfading to avoid the volume dip at center position.
Hold [SCENE A] and check [TRACK] and [TRACK PARAMETER] key brightness to see which tracks/pages have scene locks.
Muting a scene ([FUNC]+[SCENE]) is great for toggling effect washes on/off during performance.

scene_b.label: SCENE B
scene_b.primary: Press [SCENE B] + [TRIG 1-16] to assign a scene to the B slot of the crossfader (fully active at rightmost crossfader position). Hold [SCENE B] and turn encoders to lock parameter values to that scene.
scene_b.func_combo: [FUNC] + [SCENE B] = Mute Scene B. Muted scene parameters are ignored, using the part's default values instead.
all_combos: [SCENE B] + [TRIG] = Assign scene to slot B
[SCENE B] + encoder turn = Lock parameter to Scene B
[SCENE B] + encoder press = Remove scene lock for that parameter
[SCENE B] + LEVEL turn = Lock XLV to Scene B
[SCENE B] + LEVEL press = Remove XLV lock
[FUNC] + [SCENE B] = Mute/unmute Scene B
[SCENE B] + [RECORD] = Copy Scene B
[SCENE B] + [PLAY] = Clear Scene B locks
[SCENE B] + [STOP] = Paste to Scene B
scene_b.menu_path: N/A — scene assignment and locking key.
parameters: Same scene-exclusive parameters as Scene A (XLV, XVOL, XDIR)
scene_b.leds: When [SCENE B] is pressed: red [TRIG] = currently assigned scene, green [TRIG] = Scene A's assignment, half-bright green = scenes containing locks.
tips: Same tips as Scene A. A common setup: Scene A = clean/dry, Scene B = heavy effects/tension.
16 scenes per Part — plenty of room for variation. Scenes are part of the Part, not the Pattern.

crossfader.label: CROSSFADER
crossfader.primary: Interpolates between the parameter values of Scene A (leftmost) and Scene B (rightmost). Any crossfader position between the extremes blends the two scenes proportionally.
crossfader.func_combo: N/A — physical slider, no FUNC combination.
crossfader.menu_path: N/A — physical control. Crossfader position shown at bottom-right of the screen.
crossfader.leds: No LED. Position indicated on screen.
tips: Scene locks override parameter locks — the crossfader always takes priority.
If two empty scenes are assigned, the crossfader does nothing.
The crossfader center position has a natural volume dip — use XLV/XVOL for equal-energy crossfading.
The crossfader can control ANY lockable parameter across ALL 8 tracks simultaneously.
The MKII crossfader is upgraded with a smoother, silkier action compared to the MKI.

trig_1.label: TRIG 1
trig_1.primary: Multi-purpose key. In Grid Recording: places/removes trigs on step 1. With [BANK]: selects Bank A. With [PTN]: selects Pattern 1. With [SCENE A/B]: assigns Scene 1. In Tracks mode (no recording): trigs Track 1 if disconnected.
trig_1.func_combo: [FUNC] + [TRIG 1] = Place lock trig on step 1 (Grid Recording) / One-shot trig toggle.
all_combos: [TRIG 1] = Place/remove trig on step 1 (Grid Rec) / Trig disconnected Track 1 (Tracks mode)
[BANK] + [TRIG 1] = Select Bank A
[PTN] + [TRIG 1] = Select Pattern 1 in current bank
[SCENE A/B] + [TRIG 1] = Assign Scene 1 to slot A or B
[FUNC] + [TRIG 1] = Place lock trig / Toggle one-shot (Grid Rec)
[TRIG 1] + encoder turn = Parameter lock for step 1
[TRIG 1] + [UP/DOWN] = Sample Lock menu (Grid Rec)
[TRIG 1] + [LEFT/RIGHT] = Micro Timing menu (Grid Rec)
[TRIG 1] + [YES] = Preview trig on main outputs
[TRIG 1] + [CUE] = Preview trig on cue outputs
[TRIG 1] + [RECORD] = Copy trig
[TRIG 1] + [PLAY] = Clear trig locks
[TRIG 1] + [STOP] = Paste trig
[TRIG 1] + [NO] = Convert trig type (sample→trigless→lock cycle)
trig_1.leds: Red = sample trig. Flashing red = sample trig with p-lock or sample lock. Green (bright) = trigless trig. Green (dim) = lock trig. Yellow = one-shot trig. Yellow/red alternating = fired one-shot. Unlit = no trig.
tips: In LIVE RECORDING mode, [TRIG 9-16] input sample trigs on tracks 1-8 (not [TRIG 1-8]).
In Chromatic mode, TRIG keys become a mini-keyboard. Yellow = C notes, red = white keys, unlit = black keys.
In Quick Mute mode, [TRIG 1-8] mute audio tracks, [TRIG 9-16] mute MIDI tracks.
Hold a trig and turn LEVEL knob to open the Sample Lock browser.

trig_2.label: TRIG 2
trig_2.primary: Same as TRIG 1 but for step 2 / Bank B / Pattern 2 / Scene 2.
trig_2.func_combo: [FUNC] + [TRIG 2] = Lock trig / one-shot toggle on step 2.
all_combos: Same combos as TRIG 1, applied to position 2: [BANK]+[TRIG 2]=Bank B, [PTN]+[TRIG 2]=Pattern 2, etc.
trig_2.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.
tips: [TRIG 2] in Startup Menu = EMPTY RESET (reinitializes battery-backed RAM, clears internal settings, CF card unaffected).

trig_3.label: TRIG 3
trig_3.primary: Same as TRIG 1 but for step 3 / Bank C / Pattern 3 / Scene 3.
trig_3.func_combo: [FUNC] + [TRIG 3] = Lock trig / one-shot toggle on step 3.
all_combos: Same combos as TRIG 1, applied to position 3.
trig_3.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.

trig_4.label: TRIG 4
trig_4.primary: Same as TRIG 1 but for step 4 / Bank D / Pattern 4 / Scene 4.
trig_4.func_combo: [FUNC] + [TRIG 4] = Lock trig / one-shot toggle on step 4.
all_combos: Same combos as TRIG 1, applied to position 4.
trig_4.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.

trig_5.label: TRIG 5
trig_5.primary: Same as TRIG 1 but for step 5 / Bank E / Pattern 5 / Scene 5.
trig_5.func_combo: [FUNC] + [TRIG 5] = Lock trig / one-shot toggle on step 5.
all_combos: Same combos as TRIG 1, applied to position 5.
trig_5.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.

trig_6.label: TRIG 6
trig_6.primary: Same as TRIG 1 but for step 6 / Bank F / Pattern 6 / Scene 6.
trig_6.func_combo: [FUNC] + [TRIG 6] = Lock trig / one-shot toggle on step 6.
all_combos: Same combos as TRIG 1, applied to position 6.
trig_6.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.

trig_7.label: TRIG 7
trig_7.primary: Same as TRIG 1 but for step 7 / Bank G / Pattern 7 / Scene 7.
trig_7.func_combo: [FUNC] + [TRIG 7] = Lock trig / one-shot toggle on step 7.
all_combos: Same combos as TRIG 1, applied to position 7.
trig_7.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.

trig_8.label: TRIG 8
trig_8.primary: Same as TRIG 1 but for step 8 / Bank H / Pattern 8 / Scene 8.
trig_8.func_combo: [FUNC] + [TRIG 8] = Lock trig / one-shot toggle on step 8.
all_combos: Same combos as TRIG 1, applied to position 8.
trig_8.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.

trig_9.label: TRIG 9
trig_9.primary: Step 9 in Grid Recording. In TRACKS mode without recording, trigs Track 1's machine. In LIVE RECORDING, inputs a sample trig on Track 1. With [BANK]: selects Bank I. With [PTN]: Pattern 9.
trig_9.func_combo: [FUNC] + [TRIG 9] = Lock trig on step 9 / Manually trig trigless trig on Track 1 (no recording mode, Tracks/Chromatic).
all_combos: Same combos as TRIG 1 applied to position 9. [BANK]+[TRIG 9]=Bank I. In Tracks mode: trigs Track 1 machine. In Live Recording: places sample trig on Track 1.
trig_9.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys. Outside Grid Rec, flashes when Track 1's trig fires.
tips: In LIVE RECORDING, [TRIG 9-16] are the primary way to input sample trigs on tracks 1-8 in real time.
In TRACKS mode (no recording), [TRIG 9] plays Track 1's sample like pressing a drum pad.

trig_10.label: TRIG 10
trig_10.primary: Step 10 / Bank J / Pattern 10. In Tracks mode, trigs Track 2. In Live Recording, inputs trig on Track 2.
all_combos: Same as TRIG 9, applied to position 10 / Track 2.
trig_10.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.

trig_11.label: TRIG 11
trig_11.primary: Step 11 / Bank K / Pattern 11. In Tracks mode, trigs Track 3. In Live Recording, inputs trig on Track 3.
all_combos: Same as TRIG 9, applied to position 11 / Track 3.
trig_11.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.

trig_12.label: TRIG 12
trig_12.primary: Step 12 / Bank L / Pattern 12. In Tracks mode, trigs Track 4. In Live Recording, inputs trig on Track 4.
all_combos: Same as TRIG 9, applied to position 12 / Track 4.
trig_12.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.

trig_13.label: TRIG 13
trig_13.primary: Step 13 / Bank M / Pattern 13. In Tracks mode, trigs Track 5. In Live Recording, inputs trig on Track 5. In Chromatic mode, this is the ROOT NOTE (C at octave 0).
all_combos: Same as TRIG 9, applied to position 13 / Track 5.
trig_13.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys. In Chromatic mode: yellow (C note).
tips: In CHROMATIC mode, [TRIG 13] is the root note C at octave 0 — the reference pitch of the sample.

trig_14.label: TRIG 14
trig_14.primary: Step 14 / Bank N / Pattern 14. In Tracks mode, trigs Track 6. In Live Recording, inputs trig on Track 6.
all_combos: Same as TRIG 9, applied to position 14 / Track 6.
trig_14.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.

trig_15.label: TRIG 15
trig_15.primary: Step 15 / Bank O / Pattern 15. In Tracks mode, trigs Track 7. In Live Recording, inputs trig on Track 7.
all_combos: Same as TRIG 9, applied to position 15 / Track 7.
trig_15.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.

trig_16.label: TRIG 16
trig_16.primary: Step 16 / Bank P / Pattern 16. In Tracks mode, trigs Track 8. In Live Recording, inputs trig on Track 8.
all_combos: Same as TRIG 9, applied to position 16 / Track 8.
trig_16.leds: Same LED behavior as all trig keys.

page.label: PAGE
page.primary: Selects the active pattern page when GRID RECORDING mode is active. Patterns can have 1-4 pages (16-64 steps). Also used in Slots/Slices trig modes to navigate sample slot/slice pages.
page.func_combo: [FUNC] + [PAGE] = Opens the SCALE SETUP menu where pattern/track length, time signature, and tempo multiplier are set.
all_combos: [PAGE] = Switch pattern page (in Grid Recording) / Navigate slot/slice pages (in Slots/Slices mode)
[FUNC] + [PAGE] = Open Scale Setup menu
[PAGE] + [RECORD] = Copy active pattern page
[PAGE] + [PLAY] = Clear active pattern page
[PAGE] + [STOP] = Paste to active pattern page
[PAGE] (in Chromatic mode) = Acts as an additional keyboard key
[PAGE] (in Scale Setup) = Quick-change total pattern/track length
[UP] + [PAGE] = Activate FILL mode (hold for duration)
[DOWN] + [PAGE] = Activate FILL mode for one pattern cycle
[UP] + [DOWN] + [PAGE] = Latch FILL mode
page.menu_path: SCALE SETUP menu (via FUNC+PAGE) → NORMAL mode: Pattern Scale (steps/total), Tempo Multiplier. PER TRACK mode: Scale Track, Tempo Multiplier, Master Length, Master Scale.
parameters: NORMAL: PATTERN SCALE (1-64 steps / 16,32,48,64 total), TEMPO MULTIPLIER (1/8X to 2X)
PER TRACK: SCALE TRACK (per-track length), TEMPO MULTIPLIER (per-track), MASTER LENGTH (overall pattern restart), MASTER SCALE (overall time signature)
page.leds: Four <PAGE> LEDs above the [PAGE] key indicate which page is active/playing. For 16-step patterns, only LED 1:4 is lit and [PAGE] has no effect.
tips: Trigs are auto-copied when extending pattern length (e.g., extending from 16 to 32 steps copies page 1 to page 2). Disable in Personalize.
In Scale Setup, press [TRIG] keys to quickly set the number of steps; press [PAGE] to quickly set total length.
FILL mode ([UP/DOWN]+[PAGE]) is essential for drum fills and conditional trig patterns.
2X tempo multiplier doubles the step resolution to 32nd notes — great for detailed hi-hat patterns.

encoder_level.label: LEVEL (Main Encoder)
encoder_level.primary: Sets the overall post-effects volume level of the active track (routed to main outputs only). Press the encoder while turning for larger value increments (steps of 7).
encoder_level.func_combo: [FUNC] + LEVEL = Adjusts the MAIN output volume parameter from the Mixer menu (global output level).
all_combos: LEVEL = Set active track's main output level (post-FX)
[FUNC] + LEVEL = Adjust global MAIN output level
[CUE] + LEVEL = Set active track's CUE output level
[SCENE A/B] + LEVEL = Lock XLV parameter to scene (equal-energy track volume)
LEVEL (in Tempo menu) = Change BPM
LEVEL (in Mixer menu) = Change parameter values
LEVEL (in menus/lists) = Navigate/change values
LEVEL (in Naming menu) = Select characters
LEVEL (in Sample Lock menu) = Navigate sample list
LEVEL (in Micro Timing) = Select trig condition
encoder_level.leds: No LED. Level bar shown on the left side of the screen display.
tips: LEVEL is post-effects; VOL on the AMP page is pre-effects. Use both for flexible volume control.
LEVEL only affects the main output routing. Use [CUE]+LEVEL for the cue output level.
Press the encoder while turning for quick parameter sweeps (increments of 7).
The LEVEL encoder is incredibly versatile — it adjusts different parameters depending on which menu is open.

encoder_a.label: DATA ENTRY A
encoder_a.primary: Controls the upper-left parameter on the screen. In track parameter pages, this is typically the first parameter (e.g., PTCH on SRC, ATK on AMP, SPD1 on LFO). Press while turning for larger increments.
encoder_a.func_combo: [FUNC] + turn = Parameter value jump (snaps to key values like 0, 64, 127).
all_combos: Turn = Adjust upper-left parameter
Press + turn = Quick parameter adjustment (increments of 7)
[FUNC] + turn = Parameter value jump
[TRIG] + turn = Parameter lock for that step
[SCENE A/B] + turn = Scene lock for that parameter
[TRIG] + press = Remove parameter lock for that parameter
tips: The physical layout of encoders A-F matches the on-screen parameter grid: A/B/C on top, D/E/F on bottom.
In the Audio Editor TRIM page: A = start point, B = loop point, C = end point.
Hold [FUNC] while pressing the encoder to enable/disable MIDI CC parameters in CTRL pages.

encoder_b.label: DATA ENTRY B
encoder_b.primary: Controls the upper-middle parameter on the screen. Press while turning for larger increments.
all_combos: Same combinations as Encoder A, applied to the upper-middle parameter position.
tips: Same tips as Encoder A.

encoder_c.label: DATA ENTRY C
encoder_c.primary: Controls the upper-right parameter on the screen. Press while turning for larger increments.
all_combos: Same combinations as Encoder A, applied to the upper-right parameter position.
tips: Same tips as Encoder A.

tap_tempo.label: TEMPO
tap_tempo.primary: Opens the TEMPO menu where the project BPM is set. The [TEMPO] key LED flashes at the current tempo speed, providing a visual metronome.
tap_tempo.func_combo: [FUNC] + [TEMPO] = Tap Tempo. Hold [FUNC] and tap [TEMPO] repeatedly in rhythm. After 4 taps, the average BPM is calculated and applied. Continue tapping to refine.
all_combos: [TEMPO] = Open Tempo menu
[FUNC] + [TEMPO] (repeated taps) = Tap tempo
[CUE] + [TEMPO] = Toggle metronome on/off
[TRACK] + [TEMPO] = Sync sequencer to Pickup machine loop on that track
[FUNC] + [YES] (in Tempo menu) = Switch between PROJECT and PATTERN tempo mode
tap_tempo.menu_path: TEMPO menu → BPM setting (LEVEL knob for integer, [UP/DOWN] for decimal). PROJECT or PATTERN tempo mode.
parameters: Mode: PROJECT (shared BPM for all patterns) or PATTERN (per-pattern BPM)
tap_tempo.leds: [TEMPO] key LED flashes at the current BPM speed — always visible as a tempo indicator.
tips: Tap tempo needs at least 4 taps before setting the BPM.
Press [LEFT/RIGHT] arrows (no menu open) to temporarily nudge BPM +/- 10% for manual syncing.
PATTERN tempo mode allows each pattern to have its own BPM — great for songs with tempo changes.
Arranger row BPM settings override both project and pattern tempo.

encoder_d.label: DATA ENTRY D
encoder_d.primary: Controls the lower-left parameter on the screen. Press while turning for larger increments.
all_combos: Same combinations as Encoder A, applied to the lower-left parameter position.
tips: Same tips as Encoder A. In Audio Editor TRIM page: D = y-axis zoom.

encoder_e.label: DATA ENTRY E
encoder_e.primary: Controls the lower-middle parameter on the screen. Press while turning for larger increments.
all_combos: Same combinations as Encoder A, applied to the lower-middle parameter position.
tips: Same tips as Encoder A. In Audio Editor TRIM page: E = waveform scroll.

encoder_f.label: DATA ENTRY F
encoder_f.primary: Controls the lower-right parameter on the screen. Press while turning for larger increments.
all_combos: Same combinations as Encoder A, applied to the lower-right parameter position.
tips: Same tips as Encoder A. In Audio Editor TRIM page: F = x-axis zoom. In Arpeggiator Setup: F = key scale.

headphone_vol.label: HEADPHONES VOL
headphone_vol.primary: Dedicated encoder that sets the volume for the stereo headphones output jack on the rear panel. Independent of main output volume.
headphone_vol.menu_path: N/A — hardware volume control for headphone output.
tips: The headphone output mirrors either the main outputs, the cue outputs, or a mix of both — controlled by the MIX parameter in the Mixer menu.
Independent from the MAIN/CUE OUT levels in the Mixer menu.
Use with the MIX parameter to DJ-style cue preview tracks in headphones while the audience hears the main mix.
